title: चमकम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री रुद्रम् चमकम् ॥

॥ प्रथमोऽनुवाकः ॥

अग्नाविष्णू सजोषसेमा वर्धन्तु वां गिरः।
द्युम्नैर्वाजेभिरागतम्॥

वाजश्च मे प्रसवश्च मे प्रयतिश्च मे प्रसितिश्च मे धीतिश्च मे क्रतुश्च मे
स्वरश्च मे श्लोकश्च मे श्रावश्च मे श्रुतिश्च मे ज्योतिश्च मे सुवश्च मे
प्राणश्च मेऽपानश्च मे व्यानश्च मेऽसुश्च मे चित्तं च म
आधीतं च मे वाक्च मे मनश्च मे चक्षुश्च मे श्रोत्रं च मे
दक्षश्च मे बलं च म ओजश्च मे सहश्च म
आयुश्च मे जरा च म आत्मा च मे तनूश्च मे शर्म च मे वर्म च मे
ऽङ्गानि च मेऽस्थानि च मे परूंषि च मे शरीराणि च मे॥ १॥

॥ द्वितीयोऽनुवाकः ॥

ज्यैष्ठ्यं च म आधिपत्यं च मे मन्युश्च मे भामश्च मेऽमश्च मेऽम्भश्च मे
जेमा च मे महिमा च मे वरिमा च मे प्रथिमा च मे
वर्ष्मा च मे द्राघिमा च मे वृद्धं च मे वृद्धिश्च मे सत्यं च मे श्रद्धा च मे
जगच्च मे धनं च मे वशश्च मे त्विषिश्च मे क्रीडा च मे मोदश्च मे
जातं च मे जनिष्यमाणं च मे सूक्तं च मे सुकृतं च मे
वित्तं च मे वेद्यं च मे भूतं च मे भविष्यच्च मे
सुगं च मे सुपथं च म ऋद्धं च म ऋद्धिश्च मे
क्लृप्तं च मे क्लृप्तिश्च मे मतिश्च मे सुमतिश्च मे॥ २॥

॥ तृतीयोऽनुवाकः ॥

शं च मे मयश्च मे प्रियं च मेऽनुकामश्च मे कामश्च मे
सौमनसश्च मे भद्रं च मे श्रेयश्च मे वस्यश्च मे यशश्च मे
भगश्च मे द्रविणं च मे यन्ता च मे धर्ता च मे क्षेमश्च मे धृतिश्च मे
विश्वं च मे महश्च मे संवित्च मे ज्ञात्रं च मे सूश्च मे प्रसूश्च मे
सीरं च मे लयश्च म ऋतं च मेऽमृतं च मे
अयक्ष्मं च मेऽनामयच्च मे जीवातुश्च मे दीर्घायुत्वं च मे
अनमित्रं च मेऽभयं च मे सुगं च मे शयनं च मे
सूषा च मे सुदिनं च मे॥ ३॥

॥ चतुर्थोऽनुवाकः ॥

ऊर्क्च मे सूनृता च मे पयश्च मे रसश्च मे घृतं च मे मधु च मे
सग्धिश्च मे सपीतिश्च मे कृषिश्च मे वृष्टिश्च मे जैत्रं च म
औद्भिद्यं च मे रयिश्च मे रायश्च मे पुष्टं च मे पुष्टिश्च मे
विभु च मे प्रभु च मे बहु च मे भूयश्च मे
पूर्णं च मे पूर्णतरं च मेऽक्षितिश्च मे कूयवाश्च मे
अन्नं च मेऽक्षुच्च मे व्रीहयश्च मे यवाश्च मे
माषाश्च मे तिलाश्च मे मुद्गाश्च मे खल्वाश्च मे
गोधूमाश्च मे मसुराश्च मे प्रियङ्गवश्च मेऽणवश्च मे
श्यामाकाश्च मे नीवाराश्च मे॥ ४॥

॥ पञ्चमोऽनुवाकः ॥

अश्मा च मे मृत्तिका च मे गिरयश्च मे पर्वताश्च मे
सिकताश्च मे वनस्पतयश्च मे हिरण्यं च मेऽयश्च मे
सीसं च मे त्रपुश्च मे श्यामं च मे लोहं च मेऽग्निश्च म
आपश्च मे वीरुधश्च म ओषधयश्च मे कृष्टपच्यं च मेऽकृष्टपच्यं च मे
ग्राम्याश्च मे पशव आरण्याश्च
यज्ञेन कल्पन्तां वित्तं च मे वित्तिश्च मे
भूतं च मे भूतिश्च मे वसु च मे वसतिश्च मे
कर्म च मे शक्तिश्च मेऽर्थश्च म एमश्च म इतिश्च मे गतिश्च मे॥ ५॥

॥ षष्ठोऽनुवाकः ॥

अग्निश्च म इन्द्रश्च मे सोमश्च म इन्द्रश्च मे सविता च म इन्द्रश्च मे
सरस्वती च म इन्द्रश्च मे पूषा च म इन्द्रश्च मे बृहस्पतिश्च म इन्द्रश्च मे
मित्रश्च म इन्द्रश्च मे वरुणश्च म इन्द्रश्च मे त्वष्टा च म इन्द्रश्च मे
धाता च म इन्द्रश्च मे विष्णुश्च म इन्द्रश्च मेऽश्विनौ च म इन्द्रश्च मे
मरुतश्च म इन्द्रश्च मे विश्वे च मे देवा इन्द्रश्च मे
पृथिवी च म इन्द्रश्च मेऽन्तरिक्षं च म इन्द्रश्च मे द्यौश्च म इन्द्रश्च मे
दिशश्च म इन्द्रश्च मे मूर्धा च म इन्द्रश्च मे
प्रजापतिश्च म इन्द्रश्च मे॥ ६॥

॥ सप्तमोऽनुवाकः ॥

अंशुश्च मे रश्मिश्च मेऽदाभ्यश्च मेऽधिपतिश्च म
उपांशुश्च मेऽन्तर्यामश्च मे ऐन्द्रवायवश्च मे मैत्रावरुणश्च मे
आश्विनश्च मे प्रतिपस्थानश्च मे शुक्रश्च मे मन्थी च म
आग्रयणश्च मे वैश्वदेवश्च मे ध्रुवश्च मे वैश्वानरश्च मे
ऋतुग्रहाश्च मेऽतिग्राह्याश्च म ऐन्द्राग्नश्च मे वैश्वदेवश्च मे
मरुत्वतीयाश्च मे माहेन्द्रश्च म
आदित्यश्च मे सावित्रश्च मे सारस्वतश्च मे पौष्णश्च मे
पात्नीवतश्च मे हारियोजनश्च मे॥ ७॥

॥ अष्टमोऽनुवाकः ॥

इध्मश्च मे बर्हिश्च मे वेदिश्च मे धिष्णियाश्च मे
स्रुचश्च मे चमसाश्च मे ग्रावाणश्च मे स्वरवश्च मे
उपरवाश्च मेऽधिषवणे च मे द्रोणकलशश्च मे वायव्यानि च मे
पूतभृच्च मेऽऽदवनीयश्च म
आग्नीध्रं च मे हविर्धानं च मे गृहाश्च मे सदश्च मे
पुरोडाशाश्च मे पचताश्च मेऽवभृथश्च मे स्वगाकारश्च मे॥ ८॥

॥ नवमोऽनुवाकः ॥

अग्निश्च मे घर्मश्च मेऽर्कश्च मे सूर्यश्च मे प्राणश्च मेऽश्वमेधश्च मे
पृथिवी च मेऽदितिश्च मे दितिश्च मे द्यौश्च मे शक्वरीरङ्गुलयो
दिशश्च मे यज्ञेन कल्पन्ताम् ऋक्च मे साम च मे स्तोमश्च मे
यजुश्च मे दीक्षा च मे तपश्च म ऋतुश्च मे व्रतं च मे
ऽहोरात्रयोर्वृष्ट्या बृहद्रथन्तरे च मे यज्ञेन कल्पेताम्॥ ९॥

॥ दशमोऽनुवाकः ॥

गर्भाश्च मे वत्साश्च मे त्र्यविश्च मे त्र्यवी च मे
दित्यवाट् च मे दित्यौही च मे पञ्चाविश्च मे पञ्चावी च मे
त्रिवत्सश्च मे त्रिवत्सा च मे तुर्यवाट् च मे तुर्यौही च मे
पष्ठवाट् च मे पष्ठौही च मे उक्षा च मे वशा च मे
ऋषभश्च मे वेहच्च मे ऽनड्वाञ्च मे धेनुश्च म
आयुर्यज्ञेन कल्पतां प्राणो यज्ञेन कल्पतामपानो यज्ञेन कल्पतां
व्यानो यज्ञेन कल्पतां चक्षुर्यज्ञेन कल्पतां श्रोत्रं यज्ञेन कल्पतां
मनो यज्ञेन कल्पतां वाग्यज्ञेन कल्पतामात्मा यज्ञेन कल्पतां
यज्ञो यज्ञेन कल्पताम्॥ १०॥

॥ एकादशोऽनुवाकः ॥

एका च मे तिस्रश्च मे पञ्च च मे सप्त च मे नव च म
एकादश च मे त्रयोदश च मे पञ्चदश च मे सप्तदश च मे
नवदश च म एकविंशतिश्च मे त्रयोविंशतिश्च मे
पञ्चविंशतिश्च मे सप्तविंशतिश्च मे नवविंशतिश्च म
एकत्रिंशच्च मे त्रयस्त्रिंशच्च मे

चतस्रश्च मेऽष्टौ च मे द्वादश च मे षोडश च मे विंशतिश्च मे
चतुर्विंशतिश्च मेऽष्टाविंशतिश्च मे द्वात्रिंशच्च मे षट्त्रिंशच्च मे
चत्वारिंशच्च मे चतुश्चत्वारिंशच्च मेऽष्टाचत्वारिंशच्च मे

वाजश्च प्रसवश्चापिजश्च क्रतुश्च सुवश्च मूर्धा च
व्यश्नियश्चान्त्यायनश्चान्त्यश्च भौवनश्च
भुवनश्चाधिपतिश्च॥ ११॥

ॐ शान्तिः शान्तिः शान्तिः॥

॥ इति श्री कृष्णयजुर्वेद तैत्तिरीयसंहिता चतुर्थकाण्डे सप्तमः प्रपाठकः चमकं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Rudram Chamakam ||

|| Prathamonuvakah ||

Agnaa-Vishnoo sajosha-sema vardhantau vaam girah |
Dyumnair-vajebhir-agatam ||

Vajash-cha me prasavash-cha me prayatish-cha me prasitish-cha me dhitish-cha me kratush-cha me
svarash-cha me shlokash-cha me shravash-cha me shrutish-cha me jyotish-cha me suvash-cha me
pranash-cha me-apanash-cha me vyanash-cha me-asush-cha me chittam cha ma
adhitam cha me vakcha me manash-cha me chakshush-cha me shrotram cha me
dakshash-cha me balam cha ma ojash-cha me sahash-cha ma
ayush-cha me jara cha ma atma cha me tanush-cha me sharma cha me varma cha me
angani cha me-asthani cha me parumshi cha me sharirani cha me || 1 ||

|| Dvitiyonuvakah ||

Jyaishthyam cha ma adhipatyam cha me manyush-cha me bhamash-cha me-amash-cha me-ambhash-cha me
jema cha me mahima cha me varima cha me prathima cha me
varshmaa cha me draghima cha me vriddham cha me vriddhish-cha me satyam cha me shraddha cha me
jagach-cha me dhanam cha me vashash-cha me tvishish-cha me krida cha me modash-cha me
jatam cha me janishyamanam cha me suktam cha me sukritam cha me
vittam cha me vedyam cha me bhutam cha me bhavishyach-cha me
sugam cha me supatham cha ma riddham cha ma riddhish-cha me
klriptam cha me klriptish-cha me matish-cha me sumatish-cha me || 2 ||

|| Tritiyonuvakah ||

Sham cha me mayash-cha me priyam cha me-anukamash-cha me kamash-cha me
saumanasash-cha me bhadram cha me shreyash-cha me vasyash-cha me yashash-cha me
bhagash-cha me dravinam cha me yanta cha me dharta cha me kshemash-cha me dhritish-cha me
vishvam cha me mahash-cha me samvit-cha me jnatram cha me sush-cha me prasush-cha me
siram cha me layash-cha ma ritam cha me-amritam cha me
ayakshmam cha me-anamayach-cha me jivatush-cha me dirghayutvam cha me
anamitram cha me-abhayam cha me sugam cha me shayanam cha me
susha cha me sudinam cha me || 3 ||

|| Chaturthonuvakah ||

Urkcha me sunrita cha me payash-cha me rasash-cha me ghritam cha me madhu cha me
sagdhish-cha me sapitish-cha me krishish-cha me vrishtish-cha me jaitram cha ma
audbhidyam cha me rayish-cha me rayash-cha me pushtam cha me pushtish-cha me
vibhu cha me prabhu cha me bahu cha me bhuyash-cha me
purnam cha me purnataram cha me-akshitish-cha me kuyavash-cha me
annam cha me-akshuch-cha me vrihayash-cha me yavash-cha me
mashash-cha me tilash-cha me mudgash-cha me khalvash-cha me
godhuma-shcha me masurash-cha me priyangavash-cha me-anavash-cha me
shyamakash-cha me nivarash-cha me || 4 ||

|| Panchamonuvakah ||

Ashma cha me mrittika cha me girayash-cha me parvatash-cha me
sikatash-cha me vanaspatayash-cha me hiranyam cha me-ayash-cha me
sisam cha me trapush-cha me shyamam cha me loham cha me-agnish-cha ma
apash-cha me virudhash-cha ma oshadhayash-cha me krishtapachyam cha me-akrishtapachyam cha me
gramyash-cha me pashava aranyash-cha
yajnena kalpantam vittam cha me vittish-cha me
bhutam cha me bhutish-cha me vasu cha me vasatish-cha me
karma cha me shaktish-cha me-arthash-cha ma emash-cha ma itish-cha me gatish-cha me || 5 ||

|| Shashthonuvakah ||

Agnish-cha ma Indrash-cha me Somash-cha ma Indrash-cha me Savita cha ma Indrash-cha me
Sarasvati cha ma Indrash-cha me Pusha cha ma Indrash-cha me Brihaspatish-cha ma Indrash-cha me
Mitrash-cha ma Indrash-cha me Varunash-cha ma Indrash-cha me Tvashta cha ma Indrash-cha me
Dhata cha ma Indrash-cha me Vishnush-cha ma Indrash-cha me-Ashvinau cha ma Indrash-cha me
Marutash-cha ma Indrash-cha me Vishve cha me Deva Indrash-cha me
Prithivi cha ma Indrash-cha me-Antarksham cha ma Indrash-cha me Dyaush-cha ma Indrash-cha me
Dishash-cha ma Indrash-cha me Murdha cha ma Indrash-cha me
Prajapatish-cha ma Indrash-cha me || 6 ||

|| Saptamonuvakah ||

Amshush-cha me rashmish-cha me-adabhyash-cha me-adhipatish-cha ma
upamshush-cha me-antaryamash-cha me Aindravayavash-cha me Maitravarunash-cha me
Ashvinash-cha me pratipasthanash-cha me Shukrash-cha me Manthi cha ma
Agrayanash-cha me Vaishvadevash-cha me Dhruvash-cha me Vaishvanarash-cha me
Ritugrahash-cha me-atigrahyash-cha ma Aindragnash-cha me Vaishvadevash-cha me
Marutvatiyash-cha me Mahendrash-cha ma
Adityash-cha me Savitrash-cha me Sarasvatash-cha me Paushnash-cha me
Patnivatash-cha me Hariyojanash-cha me || 7 ||

|| Ashtamonuvakah ||

Idhmash-cha me barhish-cha me vedish-cha me dhishniyash-cha me
sruchash-cha me chamasash-cha me gravanash-cha me svaravash-cha me
uparavash-cha me-adhishavane cha me dronakalashash-cha me vayavyani cha me
putabhritch-cha me-adavaniyash-cha ma
Agnnidhram cha me havirddhanam cha me grihash-cha me sadash-cha me
purodasash-cha me pachatash-cha me-avabhrithash-cha me svagakarash-cha me || 8 ||

|| Navamonuvakah ||

Agnish-cha me gharmash-cha me-arkash-cha me Suryash-cha me pranash-cha me-ashvamedhash-cha me
Prithivi cha me-Aditish-cha me Ditish-cha me Dyaush-cha me shakvarirangulayo
dishash-cha me yajnena kalpantam Rikcha me Sama cha me Stomash-cha me
yajush-cha me diksha cha me tapash-cha ma ritush-cha me vratam cha me
ahoratrayorvrishtya Brihadrathantare cha me yajnena kalpetam || 9 ||

|| Dashamonuvakah ||

Garbhash-cha me vatsash-cha me tryavish-cha me tryavi cha me
dityavat cha me dityauhi cha me panchavish-cha me panchavi cha me
trivatsash-cha me trivatsa cha me turyavat cha me turyauhi cha me
pashthavat cha me pashthauhi cha me uksha cha me vasha cha me
rishabhash-cha me vehach-cha me-anadvañ-cha me dhenush-cha ma
ayur-yajnena kalpatam prano yajnena kalpatam-apano yajnena kalpatam
vyano yajnena kalpatam chakshur-yajnena kalpatam shrotram yajnena kalpatam
mano yajnena kalpatam vag-yajnena kalpatam-atma yajnena kalpatam
yajno yajnena kalpatam || 10 ||

|| Ekadasho-nuvakah ||

Eka cha me tisrash-cha me pancha cha me sapta cha me nava cha ma
ekadasha cha me trayodasha cha me panchadasha cha me saptadasha cha me
navadasha cha ma ekavimsha-tish-cha me trayovimsha-tish-cha me
panchavimsha-tish-cha me saptavimsha-tish-cha me navavimsha-tish-cha ma
ekatrimshach-cha me trayastrisha-chcha me

Chatasrash-cha me-ashtau cha me dvadasha cha me shodasha cha me vimshatish-cha me
chaturvimsha-tish-cha me-ashtavimsha-tish-cha me dvatrimshach-cha me shattrimshach-cha me
chatvarimshach-cha me chatush-chatvarimshach-cha me-ashta-chatvarimshach-cha me

Vajash-cha prasavash-cha-apijash-cha kratush-cha suvash-cha murdha cha
vyashniyash-cha-antyayanash-cha-antyash-cha bhauvanash-cha
bhuvanash-cha-adhipatish-cha || 11 ||

Om Shantih Shantih Shantih ||

|| Iti Shri Krishna Yajurveda Taittiriya Samhita Chaturthakande Saptamah Prapathakah Chamakam Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
हे अग्नि और विष्णु! आप दोनों मिलकर मेरी स्तुतियों को बढ़ाएँ। यश और बल लेकर आइए।

मुझे बल मिले, प्रेरणा मिले, उद्यम मिले, गति मिले, बुद्धि मिले, कर्म मिले, स्वर मिले, यश मिले, श्रवण मिले, ज्ञान मिले, ज्योति मिले, सूर्य मिले, प्राण मिले, अपान मिले, व्यान मिले, जीवन मिले, चित्त मिले, वेदाध्ययन मिले, वाणी मिले, मन मिले, नेत्र मिले, कान मिले, दक्षता मिले, बल मिले, ओज मिले, सहनशक्ति मिले, आयु मिले, वृद्धावस्था मिले, आत्मा मिले, शरीर मिले, सुख मिले, कवच मिले, अंग मिले, हड्डियाँ मिलें, जोड़ मिलें, शरीर मिले॥

मुझे श्रेष्ठता मिले, अधिपत्य मिले, सत्य मिले, श्रद्धा मिले, जगत मिले, धन मिले, वश मिले, तेज मिले, क्रीड़ा मिले, मोद मिले।

मुझे सुख मिले, आनन्द मिले, प्रिय मिले, कामना की पूर्ति मिले, सौम्यता मिले, कल्याण मिले, श्रेय मिले, बेहतर मिले, यश मिले, भाग्य मिले, धन मिले, रक्षक मिले, धारक मिले, क्षेम मिले, धृति मिले। रोगरहितता मिले, निरामयता मिले, जीवन मिले, दीर्घायु मिले, शत्रुरहितता मिले, अभय मिले।

मुझे अन्न मिले, चावल मिले, जौ मिले, उड़द मिले, तिल मिले, मूंग मिले, चने मिले, गेहूँ मिले, मसूर मिले।

सोना मिले, लोहा मिले, सीसा मिले, ताँबा मिले, अग्नि मिले, जल मिले, वनस्पति मिले, औषधि मिले, पशु मिले।

एक मिले, तीन मिले, पाँच मिले, सात मिले, नौ मिले, ग्यारह मिले — यह विषम संख्याओं और सम संख्याओं की याचना है जो समस्त ब्रह्माण्डीय शक्तियों का प्रतीक है।

viniyog.kilak: श्री रुद्र प्रीत्यर्थे चमक जपे विनियोगः
viniyog.shloka: ॐ अस्य श्रीचमकमन्त्रस्य कृष्णद्वैपायनव्यास ऋषिः। अनुष्टुप् त्रिष्टुप् छन्दांसि। श्रीरुद्र (शिव) देवता। ॐ नमः शिवाय बीजम्। रुद्रशक्तिः शक्तिः। श्रीरुद्रप्रीत्यर्थे चमकजपे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Companion to Namakam - together they form the complete Sri Rudram
Invokes every conceivable blessing from Lord Rudra
Invokes material prosperity - grains, gold, cattle, and all resources
Invokes the blessing of strength of body, mind, senses, and spirit
Traditionally associated with long life, health, and freedom from disease
The mystical odd-even number sequence invokes cosmic order
Chanting with Namakam completes the Rudra Yaga
description: Chamakam (चमकम्) is a sacred prayer dedicated to Lord Shiva. Drawn from Krishna Yajurveda - Taittiriya Samhita (4.7), this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to companion to Namakam - together they form the complete Sri Rudram as well as bestow strength of body, mind, senses, and spirit. Additionally, it is known to grant material prosperity - grains, gold, cattle, and all resources. This prayer is traditionally recited on Monday (Somvar) and during Maha Shivaratri and Pradosh to invoke divine grace. Comprising 55 verses, it can be completed in approximately 25 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Chamakam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Chamakam from Krishna Yajurveda in Sanskrit with transliteration and Hindi meaning. Companion to Sri Rudram for invoking all blessings.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Chamakam and where is it preserved?
seoFaqs.answer: The Chamakam (चमकम् — also called Chamaka Prashna) is one of the supreme Vedic hymns of the Krishna Yajurveda, preserved in the Taittiriya Samhita (4.7), and is the second half of the great Sri Rudram chant — the first half being the Namakam (Taittiriya Samhita 4.5). The hymn consists of eleven anuvakas (sections) in which the worshipper, having praised Lord Rudra-Shiva in the Namakam, now boldly asks for everything he needs — both material and spiritual — using the repeated formula cha me, cha me ("and unto me, and unto me"), which gives the hymn its name (Chamaka = the section of cha-me). The Chamakam invokes Rudra to grant a vast catalogue of blessings: food, water, life, longevity, progeny, cattle, gold, prosperity, agricultural abundance, intelligence, immortality, fearlessness, all 33 devas, all sacrificial implements, and supreme spiritual realization. (Source: Krishna Yajurveda Taittiriya Samhita 4.7.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the relationship between Namakam and Chamakam?
seoFaqs.answer: The Namakam (Taittiriya Samhita 4.5) and the Chamakam (Taittiriya Samhita 4.7) together constitute the Sri Rudram (also called Sri Rudra-Adhyaya) — the central and most sacred Vedic hymn to Lord Rudra-Shiva. The Namakam is named for the repeated namo namah (salutations) by which the worshipper offers prayer and reverence to every aspect, name and form of Rudra — making it a hymn of praise and propitiation. The Chamakam is named for the repeated cha me cha me (and to me) by which the worshipper, having appeased Rudra in the Namakam, now requests his bountiful gifts — making it a hymn of petition and blessing. The two together form a complete liturgical cycle: praise (Namakam) followed by petition (Chamakam) — analogous to the Gayatri-Stotra-Phala model. They are universally recited together in Maha Rudra Yajna, Ati Rudra Yajna, Rudra Abhisheka, and daily Shaiva worship. (Source: Krishna Yajurveda; classical Shaiva Vaidika tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What does the Chamakam ask Lord Rudra to grant?
seoFaqs.answer: The Chamakam contains one of the most comprehensive catalogues of human and spiritual aspirations in all sacred literature. Across its eleven anuvakas, it asks Rudra to grant: (1) Anuvaka 1 — vital force, strength, splendour, health, sight, hearing, mind, speech and atma; (2) Anuvaka 2 — food, water, milk, ghee, honey, satisfaction, prosperity; (3) Anuvaka 3 — courage, valour, endurance, protection from enemies, victory; (4) Anuvaka 4 — gems, gold, copper, lead, tin, iron, fire, all metals; (5) Anuvaka 5 — Vedic study, mental clarity, manas, chitta, chetana; (6) Anuvaka 6 — every kind of agricultural produce, wheat, rice, barley, sesame, beans; (7) Anuvaka 7 — sacrificial implements; (8) Anuvaka 8 — yajna with all its parts; (9) Anuvaka 9 — odd-numbered Vedic measures (1, 3, 5, 7...33); (10) Anuvaka 10 — even-numbered measures (4, 8, 12...48); (11) Anuvaka 11 — final summary and supreme spiritual fulfilment. The Chamakam is the supreme Vedic prayer for both bhukti (enjoyment) and mukti (liberation). (Source: Taittiriya Samhita 4.7.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of cha me cha me — the Chamakam's signature refrain?
seoFaqs.answer: The phrase cha me (च मे) literally means "and to me" or "and unto me, this also." In each verse of the Chamakam, after a Vedic word naming a particular blessing (for example, vajash cha me = "strength to me also," prasavash cha me = "vitality to me also," prayatish cha me = "high effort to me also"), the worshipper appends cha me — bringing the listed blessing into his own life. The full benediction at the close of each anuvaka is yajnena kalpantam — "may these blessings be perfectly accomplished by the yajna." The repetition of cha me, cha me throughout the entire hymn becomes a powerful rhythmic and meditative drumbeat, training the mind to remain expansive in vision (asking Rudra for everything good) yet humble in posture (each request is a request, not a demand). The cha me formula is one of the most distinctive sounds in all Vedic chanting and is iconic of the Chamakam. (Source: Krishna Yajurveda Taittiriya Samhita 4.7; classical Vedic chanting tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the Chamakam recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Chamakam is recited on the following major occasions: (1) Daily Rudra Abhisheka — by Shaiva pujaris in every major Shiva temple worldwide, especially the twelve Jyotirlingas (Kashi Vishwanath, Mahakaleshwar, Somnath, etc.); (2) Pradosham — the thirteenth tithi of each lunar fortnight (twice a month); (3) Mondays (Somvar) — sacred to Shiva; (4) Mahashivaratri (Magha/Phalguna Krishna Chaturdashi); (5) Sawan/Shravan month (July-August), the principal monsoon month of Shiva worship in North India; (6) Maha Rudra Yajna (the chant repeated 11 times — the equivalent of one Rudra), Ati Rudra Yajna (121 times — equivalent of one Maharudra), and the supreme 14-day Ati Rudra Maha Yajna (1331 times); (7) Pranapratishtha (deity installation ceremonies); (8) Marriage ceremonies in some Smarta-Shaiva traditions; (9) Personal spiritual retreats and life-cycle samskaras. The Chamakam-Namakam pair is the gold standard of Vedic Shiva worship. (Source: Vaidika Shaiva paddhati; Drikpanchang Shiva calendar.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the benefits of reciting the Chamakam?
seoFaqs.answer: The Chamakam itself is a phalashruti — a litany of fruits to be gained — making its benefits explicit verse by verse. Tradition attributes the following: (1) sarva-bhoga-prapti — attainment of all worldly enjoyments listed in the eleven anuvakas (food, wealth, progeny, longevity, fame, courage, agricultural success, livestock, metals, gems); (2) sarva-papa-kshaya — destruction of all sins through Rudra's grace; (3) ayur-arogya-aishvarya — long life, perfect health and spiritual abundance; (4) graha-shanti — peace from planetary afflictions, especially Sani-dosha and Mangal-dosha; (5) santana-prapti — children for childless couples; (6) Vedic learning and intelligence (medha) for students; (7) victory and protection for warriors and rulers; (8) krishi-samriddhi — agricultural and economic prosperity; (9) liberation through Shiva's grace (Shiva-saayujya) — the supreme phala mentioned in the closing anuvaka. The Maha Rudra and Ati Rudra Yajnas magnify these benefits manifold for community-wide blessings. (Source: Chamakam phalashruti; Maha Rudra Yajna paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: Is the Chamakam open to all, or restricted to brahmin Vedic chanters?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditionally, the Chamakam (along with the Namakam) is a Vedic mantra-portion of the Krishna Yajurveda, and Vedic-injunction tradition (purvamimamsa) restricts its full Vedic ghana-patha (knot-pattern) recitation to qualified brahmins who have undergone Vedadhyayana (Vedic training) and upakarma. However, modern Hindu tradition, following the wider movements led by Sri Ramakrishna, Swami Vivekananda, Swami Dayananda Saraswati, the Arya Samaj, the Chinmaya Mission and numerous Shaiva and Smarta acharyas, now considers the Chamakam open to sincere devotees of all backgrounds — for sravana (listening), manana (reflection) and chanting in simple recitation form (samhita-patha). Major Shaiva temples (Pashupatinath in Kathmandu, Brihadeeswarar in Thanjavur, Meenakshi-Sundareshwarar in Madurai) freely allow lay devotees to participate in Maha Rudra Yajnas. The principle is that Rudra's grace, like all divine grace, ultimately responds to bhava (devotional intent) more than to ritual qualification. (Source: classical Mimamsa rules; modern Hindu reform traditions.)
